title: Advanced Additions Master Wave Schedule
purpose: This planning pack adds seven advanced realism systems to the existing body generation platform:

title: 01 - Physical Interaction Engine Plan
purpose: Add contact pressure, occlusion, soft-body deformation, hand placement, limb collision, furniture interaction, and body-weight realism to body image and video generation.

title: 02 - Micro-Motion Layer Plan
purpose: Add breathing, trembling, grip changes, eye movement, facial tension, skin compression motion, hair movement, and subtle muscle motion to body image/video generation.

title: 03 - Skin and Material Realism System Plan
purpose: Add pores, sweat, oil, pressure marks, redness, fluids, fabric cling, smearing, lighting response, and progressive state changes over time.

title: 04 - Fluid and Body-State Continuity Engine Plan
purpose: Track where visible fluids, sweat, makeup, hair changes, clothing removal, marks, and body state should persist across images, video clips, stitched sequences, and revisions.

title: 05 - Pose-to-Audio Force Model Plan
purpose: Drive body impacts, wet sounds, breathing, vocal effort, bed/furniture sounds, and rhythm intensity from actual pose, contact, pressure, and motion data.

title: 06 - Long-Form Fatigue and Variation Engine Plan
purpose: Prevent 5-15 minute scenes from becoming repetitive by controlling pacing variation, action variation, camera variation, vocal variation, fatigue, and intensity waves.

title: 07 - Room Acoustics and Spatial Audio Plan
purpose: Add bed/floor/wall reflections, character position panning, distance cues, muffling, reverb presets, and multi-track stems.

title: 08 - Integration, Acceptance, and Handoff
purpose: Define how the seven advanced additions integrate into the existing system and what must be true before implementation begins.

title: 09 - Deep Expansion Pass 01 Master Spec
purpose: Expand the initial advanced additions plan into a more exhaustive idea, goal, architecture, data, UI, QA, and implementation planning document.

title: 10 - Detailed 34-Wave Execution Backlog
purpose: Expand the master wave schedule into implementation-ready planning depth.

title: 11 - Canonical Artifact and Schema Blueprints
purpose: Define the concrete artifact contracts that should be created during implementation of the seven advanced realism additions.

title: 12 - Advanced Realism UI Workspace Spec
purpose: Define the operator-facing workspace for the seven advanced realism additions.

title: 13 - QA, Golden Tests, and Acceptance Rubric
purpose: Define the quality system for validating the seven advanced realism additions.

title: 14 - Deep Expansion Pass 02 Recommendations
purpose: Extend the seven advanced realism additions with deeper automation, smarter presets, additional submodules, stronger integration, and clearer implementation strategy.

title: 15 - LLM Scene Director and Auto-Generation Plan
purpose: Define how an LLM at the beginning of the system can turn a long scene writeup into a complete image, video, audio, stitching, QA, and revision plan.

title: 16 - Auto-Preset Generation and Preset Library Plan
purpose: Define how presets are created, stored, generated automatically, blended, reused, and improved over time for the seven advanced realism additions.

title: 17 - Seven Module Feature Upgrade Matrix
purpose: Add more suggested features, implementation details, automation behavior, setup requirements, and integration hooks for each of the seven advanced realism modules.

title: 18 - Proxy Preview, Animatic, and Realism Budget Plan
purpose: Define a low-cost preview system and runtime quality budget system for the seven advanced realism additions.

title: 19 - State Diff, Take Variant, and Revision Expansion
purpose: Expand the advanced realism revision system with planned/generated state comparison, take management, variant generation, and targeted rerun strategies.
